topic: Validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop: structural layout, bidirectional path-coupling repair, the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow, reference migration across commands/agents/READMEs/advisor-corpus, and whether fallback-router.ts should be wired for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback.

detail: Bound artifact_dir directly to config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir; skipped resolveArtifactRoot and all writes outside the lineage directory.

normalized_topic: validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop structural layout bidirectional path-coupling repair the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow reference migration across commands agents readmes advisor-corpus and whether fallback-router ts should be wired for real glm-5 2 to mimo-v2 5-pro fallback

focus: Establish charter, scope, and phase decomposition
noveltyJustification: First pass established the packet scope, the read-only constraint, and the five stress-test axes.
keyQuestions: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow accounted for?
Are all external references and advisor surfaces covered?
Should fallback-router.ts be wired for GLM-5.2 to MiMo fallback?
answeredQuestions: Research charter and scope confirmed from the packet.

keyFindings.title: Reference research is explicitly read-only and must feed 002/003 rather than mutate the merge itself
keyFindings.summary: The packet says the research fanout validates the merge design and explicitly excludes executing the merge or modifying deep-loop-workflows/deep-loop-runtime.

focus: Validate structural layout and runtime nesting
noveltyJustification: Confirmed the proposed topology and found one category-error guard worth preserving in implementation.
answeredQuestions: Structural layout is sound if runtime remains infrastructure, not a workflowMode.

keyFindings.title: runtime/ must be infrastructure, not an eighth mode
keyFindings.summary: The proposed tree nests the former runtime under system-deep-loop/runtime and explicitly says adding runtime as a mode-registry entry would be a category error.

noveltyJustification: Confirmed the asymmetric path rule and elevated it as the central execution invariant.
answeredQuestions: The forward and reverse directions require different repairs, not one global substitution.

keyFindings.title: Path repair is intentionally asymmetric
keyFindings.summary: Forward runtime-to-workflow references keep hop count and delete the old workflow segment; reverse workflow-to-runtime references move one hop nearer and rename the segment.

focus: Verify the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow is load-bearing
noveltyJustification: Connected the phase plan to the live package and vitest config paths that will break after nesting if not repaired.
keyQuestions: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow accounted for?
answeredQuestions: The tooling-borrow is correctly scoped into 002 and includes both runtime and system-spec-kit sides.

keyFindings.title: The tooling-borrow is not documentation; it gates runtime typecheck and council tests
keyFindings.summary: Runtime package scripts borrow tsc from system-spec-kit, tsconfig borrows @types from system-spec-kit, and system-spec-kit vitest includes deep-loop-runtime tests by relative path.

focus: Inventory executable command and agent reference migration surfaces
noveltyJustification: Expanded external migration beyond docs into command assets, doctor routes, and mirrored agents.
answeredQuestions: Commands, doctor routes, and both agent trees are explicit migration surfaces.

keyFindings.title: External migration is executable, not just prose
keyFindings.summary: Doctor deep-loop route invokes runtime scripts directly, deep command YAMLs cite both workflow and runtime paths, and OpenCode/Claude agent mirrors carry registry and script references.

focus: Stress-test advisor projection, corpus, and graph migration
noveltyJustification: Found the highest-risk migration bundle: generated projection hash, hand constants, lexical boosts, graph edges, and divergence fixtures.
answeredQuestions: Advisor migration must be staged with baseline, codegen, drift guard, corpus, and divergence ratchet.

keyFindings.title: Advisor migration is the highest-risk reference area
keyFindings.summary: The advisor hardcodes the deep-loop mode registry path, carries generated alias projection and hash, defines MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID, and has separate explicit scoring literals that still target deep-loop-workflows.

focus: Evaluate fallback-router wiring for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro
noveltyJustification: Confirmed the router can enforce approved cross-pool fallback, but the fanout pool currently only retries same lineage attempts.
keyQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired for GLM-5.2 to MiMo fallback?
answeredQuestions: Fallback wiring is valuable but should remain optional unless the operator accepts feature scope.

keyFindings.title: fallback-router.ts is ready for a real caller but fanout-pool does not call it today
keyFindings.summary: fallback-router resolves approved cross-pool fallbacks, while fanout-pool only schedules retries and marks retry_exhausted after maxRetries. The optional phase's success criterion is simply making fallback-router.ts have a non-test caller.

focus: Check GLM and MiMo model registry facts against the fallback question
noveltyJustification: Mostly confirmed known fallback constraints with model-profile evidence and clarified the approved-target requirement.
keyQuestions: Can GLM-5.2 safely fall back to MiMo-v2.5-Pro?
answeredQuestions: A GLM-to-MiMo route is technically possible only after explicit registry/config approval and smoke-tested provider availability.

keyFindings.title: GLM and MiMo are separate active pools, but neither profile declares fallback_target today
keyFindings.summary: MiMo uses xiaomi-token-plan, GLM uses zai-coding-plan, and both have fallback_target null; automatic GLM-to-MiMo would be new policy, not a latent default.

focus: Validate 003 staging and residual-reference exit gates
noveltyJustification: Low novelty; the external migration plan already contains the right staging and exit gates, but they are mandatory risk controls.
keyQuestions: Are external reference migration gates sufficient?
answeredQuestions: 003's staged plan is sufficient if baseline and post-change accuracy gates are treated as blockers.

keyFindings.title: 003 has the right dependency ordering and exit gates
keyFindings.summary: The external migration plan requires grep-before/after bracketing, hardcoded constants before generated fields, command-contract regeneration, agent mirror sync, graph edge collapse, advisor corpus re-baseline, and an 11-step exit gate.

focus: Final convergence check and ranked recommendation
noveltyJustification: Only marginal new information remained; this iteration confirmed recommendations and recorded negative knowledge.
keyQuestions: Is the merge design ready to proceed?
answeredQuestions: Proceed with 002 and 003 as designed; keep 004 optional unless the operator explicitly wants automatic fallback feature work bundled.

keyFindings.title: Proceed with 002 and 003, do not silently fold optional fallback into the merge
keyFindings.summary: The core move/reference-migration plan is sound. fallback-router wiring is real but optional feature scope, with its own success criteria and open operator question.

ruledOut.reason: Forward and reverse coupling require different relative-depth repairs.
The target spec says runtime is infrastructure, not a workflowMode.

ruledOut.approach: Silently auto-wire GLM to MiMo fallback during 002/003
ruledOut.reason: Fallback routing is optional feature work and neither model profile declares that target today.

reason: Detached lineage boundary forbids writes outside artifact_dir; research.md is canonical for this lineage.
Detached lineage boundary forbids generate-context.js side effects outside artifact_dir.
